summary: Crystal‑clear Boardman soundboard captures a powerhouse show with smoking Picasso Moon, Crazy Fingers and an epic Dark Star.
review: The tape delivers pristine, board‑level clarity that lets the band’s tight playing shine – Jerry’s tone on Crazy Fingers, Women R and Dark Star is described as “on fire,” while Vince’s keyboards earn high praise.  Standout moments include Picasso Moon, a breathtaking SOTM solo, an extended Bird Song and a soaring Dark Star space, even if the second set feels a touch slower.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high marks for both performance (numerous standout songs, enthusiastic language) and recording quality (crystal‑clear boardman source); only minor criticism about pacing.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal clear, crisp mix; reviewers call it a “master” Boardman tape with no noticeable dropouts or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: West‑coast vibe – laid‑back overall with a slightly slower second set, but the high points (Picasso Moon, Dark Star space) keep the momentum strong.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for blazing guitar tone on Crazy Fingers, Women R, Dark Star and a beautiful SOTM solo.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboard contributions are highlighted as underrated and essential to the show’s texture.
